name: Dolmas (Greek Stuffed Grape Leaves)
recipeIngredient: Green onions, including the tops, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Makes about 50 dolmas
Servings:
- Several stems of fresh parsley (about 3/4 cup freshly squeezed lemon juice)
- Additional freshly squeezed lemon juice (for drizzling) or grated/minced lemon zest for garnish
1. Wash and drain the rice.
2. Heat 3 Tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the yellow onion and saute until soft but not brown, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a mixing bowl.
3. Add the drained rice, 1/2 cup of the remaining olive oil, green onions, parsley, pine nuts, cinnamon, allspice, salt, and pepper to the mixing bowl. Set aside.
4. Rinse grape leaves under running cold water to remove as much brine as possible. Pat dry and stack on a plate.
5. Place 1 leaf at a time, shiny side down, on a flat work surface. Cut off and discard the tough stem end.
6. Spoon about 1 Tbsp of the rice mixture in the center near the base of the leaf. Fold the stem end over to cover the filling, fold both sides inward lengthwise, then tightly roll the leaf toward the pointed tip end to form a compact packet. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.
7. Pour about 2 Tbsp of the remaining olive oil at the bottom of a large pot and scatter with parsley stems to prevent grape leaves from sticking. Arrange the stuffed leaves, seam side down and almost touching, on top of the parsley.
8. Drizzle the remaining 5 Tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, and 1/2 cup stock (or water) over the leaves. Top with a heat-resistant plate and weight with a heavy can to keep leaves from unwinding during cooking.
9. Cover the pot, bring to a gentle boil, reduce heat to low, and cook until rice is tender, about 1 hour. Add a little heated liquid as needed to keep dolmas moist.
10. Remove from the heat and cool in the pot.
11. Sprinkle with lemon juice to taste, garnish with lemon zest, and serve at room temperature.
